display-name.lang: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads
programme.desc: Chucklesome sequel to The Likely Lads. After five years apart, the old pals meet by chance. Terry returns to civilian life, while marriage looms for Bob.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads
programme.desc: Brilliant 'buddy sitcom' from the early 1970s. Terry's return has been less than triumphant - his parents are away, it's cold and there's no food in the house.

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: The Tooting Popular Front, along with Mum and Dad, are in for a long night when they get held up by fugitive Speed and his gun-toting comrade MacTaggart.

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: The Tooting Popular Front hire a private detective to prove Speed's innocence - but haven't they met them somewhere before.

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: Wolfie is determined to save the Tooting he loves, but the stakes are high and he is soon forced to go on the run.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads
programme.desc: Chucklesome sequel to The Likely Lads. After five years apart, the old pals meet by chance. Terry returns to civilian life, while marriage looms for Bob.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads
programme.desc: Brilliant 'buddy sitcom' from the early 1970s. Terry's return has been less than triumphant - his parents are away, it's cold and there's no food in the house.

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: The Tooting Popular Front, along with Mum and Dad, are in for a long night when they get held up by fugitive Speed and his gun-toting comrade MacTaggart.

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: The Tooting Popular Front hire a private detective to prove Speed's innocence - but haven't they met them somewhere before.

programme.title: Citizen Smith
programme.desc: Wolfie is determined to save the Tooting he loves, but the stakes are high and he is soon forced to go on the run.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads
programme.desc: Chucklesome sequel to The Likely Lads. After five years apart, the old pals meet by chance. Terry returns to civilian life, while marriage looms for Bob.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads
programme.desc: Brilliant 'buddy sitcom' from the early 1970s. Terry's return has been less than triumphant - his parents are away, it's cold and there's no food in the house.

programme.title: Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough looks at some amazing quirks of evolution. This time, we focus on the giraffe's neck and the chameleon's tongue.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from Home.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: The South Central crews help a boy who has dislocated his knee during a school rugby match and a man suffering with agonising stomach pains after eating a bacon sandwich.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: Crews help a family whose car has crashed into a ditch, a woman with fast and erratic breathing, and a partially paralysed woman who's suffered a head injury in her garden.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: Crews treat a boy hit by a car outside school, a pensioner with a huge lump on his head and a woman with suspected sepsis.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: The medics help a man bleeding profusely, another who is in agony after falling and a woman with a cripplingly painful bowel condition.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: The South Central crews help a boy who has dislocated his knee during a school rugby match and a man suffering with agonising stomach pains after eating a bacon sandwich.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: Crews help a family whose car has crashed into a ditch, a woman with fast and erratic breathing, and a partially paralysed woman who's suffered a head injury in her garden.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: Crews treat a boy hit by a car outside school, a pensioner with a huge lump on his head and a woman with suspected sepsis.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programme.desc: The medics help a man bleeding profusely, another who is in agony after falling and a woman with a cripplingly painful bowel condition.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: Brookland's Concorde 'Delta Golf' has a makeover, in time for a visit from Concorde royalty.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A volunteer is tasked with restoring a legendary Le Mans racer. A decommissioned bomb proves difficult, and can the experts revive the dormant engines of a transatlantic flyer.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: Racing driver Billy Monger gives a fellow double amputee a driving lesson and a fire engine gets pumping for the first time in years.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: The legendary Railton Napier has a service and some treasure is found in the museum tower whilst a 100-year-old push bike gets a wooden wheel built.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A Barnes Wallis catapult is fixed by volunteers, but can they bounce bombs on water like the dam busters? A Triumph car is put through its paces in a road test.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: Brookland's Concorde 'Delta Golf' has a makeover, in time for a visit from Concorde royalty.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A volunteer is tasked with restoring a legendary Le Mans racer. A decommissioned bomb proves difficult, and can the experts revive the dormant engines of a transatlantic flyer.

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: Racing driver Billy Monger gives a fellow double amputee a driving lesson and a fire engine gets pumping for the first time in years.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team restore a 1988 Golf GTI Convertible - an iconic hot hatch - along with a wooden launch boat and a catch-up with a Mk1 Austin Mini.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek gives the once-over to a Royale Formula Ford race car, and Paul is on a marathon trip to pick up a 1953 MGYB, a Moggy Van, and an '80s Yamaha 250RD motorbike.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon and Henry head to Wiltshire. Henry is feeling happy because they're going to an old WWII airfield. Simon decides to restore a 1970's caravan while Henry opts for a boat.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill: Michael Portillo explores Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the town's community spirit.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from Home.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a beachside resort with ties to the Russian mafia, a windswept base hiding lifesaving technology, and a site in Iowa plagued by its past.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a bizarre block in Taiwan resembling a giant speaker, a pioneering venture in Alaska, and an alpine village that masks a military beast.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek is on the road to pick up a funky little Nissan Figaro and a sublime Ferrari 250 GT tribute car, and Paul gets to try out his spanking new Scania T Cab.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Hitler's Island Madness: Uncovering hidden structures built by the Nazis. The first edition focuses on Guernsey, which Hitler occupied and fortified over the course of five years.

programme.title: D-Day To Berlin
programme.desc: The last few months of the war involved final battles in Germany, the assault on Berlin, Hitler's death, and the emergence of a new dictator: Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team arrives at the National Maritime Museum Cornwall in Falmouth, where objects include a diamond and gold ring with a grim history.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A second visit to the National Maritime Museum Cornwall. Items include early medical instruments.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A super-rare Renault Clio Williams 2 in F1 blue with contrasting gold alloys turns Dave Mathewson an interesting shade of green.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team snap up a slightly saggy Seventies Saab 96 - despite a stunning restoration, could it result in a record financial loss when it goes to auction.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Hitler's Island Madness: Uncovering hidden structures built by the Nazis. The first edition focuses on Guernsey, which Hitler occupied and fortified over the course of five years.

programme.title: D-Day To Berlin
programme.desc: The last few months of the war involved final battles in Germany, the assault on Berlin, Hitler's death, and the emergence of a new dictator: Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team arrives at the National Maritime Museum Cornwall in Falmouth, where objects include a diamond and gold ring with a grim history.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways in which animals battle to survive in a world of extremes, from mountain summits to deserts, polar tundra to caves.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Jack's baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek acquires a collection of pre and post war classics, including a Vincent Rapide, a 1928 Alvis, a Ford Pilot and an Indian Scout with sidecar.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A super-rare Renault Clio Williams 2 in F1 blue with contrasting gold alloys turns Dave Mathewson an interesting shade of green.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team snap up a slightly saggy Seventies Saab 96 - despite a stunning restoration, could it result in a record financial loss when it goes to auction.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek is on the road to pick up a funky little Nissan Figaro and a sublime Ferrari 250 GT tribute car, and Paul gets to try out his spanking new Scania T Cab.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The boys visit Norfolk to assist retired engineer Graham and his wife Lindsay. They discover a rare tandem disability scooter, along with a retro record player in need of TLC.
